# backend/routers/evals_model_compare.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-05T10:00:46Z

## Purpose
§68.11 Multi-model comparison router.

3 endpoints under /api/v1/insur/evals/model-compare/* federated via
core.insur_audit (surface=evals_model_compare). The POST mutates
disk (manifest persistence) so RBAC restricts it to manager/tester;
GETs are open to _READ_ROLES via the existing /evals/* catch-all.

Composes with §68.8/9/10 (this surface JOINS their data) + §38.3 +
§47.6 + §57.7 + §64.43 #7.

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `typing` import Any
- from `fastapi` import APIRouter, HTTPException, Query, Request
- from `core.insur_audit` import log_insur_access
- from `core.middleware` import current_tenant_id
- from `schemas.model_compare` import ModelCompareRequest, ModelCompareResponse
- from `services` import model_compare_service

## Inputs
(no CLI / env inputs — library module or implicit)

## Process
### Functions
- `run_compare(http_request, payload)`: Execute a multi-model comparison; persist manifest; return scorecard.
- `history(http_request, limit)`: List recent comparison runs (newest-first by manifest mtime).
- `get_comparison(http_request, comparison_id)`: Read back a persisted comparison manifest.

## Outputs
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- body tenant_id is intentionally ignored — middleware wins per §64.43 #7
